title: 10 best herbal teas for dry skin
intro_desc: Herbal teas for dry skin are infusions made from plants and flowers that are known to soothe, moisturize, and nourish dry skin. They are a natural and caffeine-free alternative to traditional teas. Some examples of herbal teas that help with dry skin include chamomile, calendula, and lavender. Chamomile tea is known for its anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce redness and irritation associated with dry skin. Calendula tea is rich in antioxidants and has been shown to promote skin hydration and elasticity. Lavender tea has calming effects and can help soothe dry, itchy skin. Drinking these herbal teas can improve the lives of those with dry skin by providing a simple, cost-effective, and natural way to alleviate dryness and discomfort. Additionally, these teas can be used topically as a compress or added to bath water for an extra boost of hydration.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: High mallow teas helps with dry skin because it contains mucilages, which are gel-like substances that provide a soothing and moisturizing effect on the skin. As you drink the tea, these mucilages are absorbed through the bloodstream and deposited into the skin, helping to lock in moisture and reduce inflammation. This can lead to improved hydration, elasticity, and flexibility of the skin, making it an effective natural remedy for dry skin conditions such as eczema, psoriasis, and dermatitis.
remedy_properties: Emollient properties: High mallow tea helps to soothe and moisturize dry, irritated skin by forming a protective barrier on the skin's surface, reducing water loss and discomfort.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in high mallow tea can help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with dry skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis.
Antioxidant properties: High mallow tea contains antioxidants that neutralize free radicals, which can cause oxidative stress and damage to the skin, leading to dryness, fine lines, and wrinkles.
remedy_parts: Roots: High in mucilage, a thick, protective substance that helps soothe and moisturize dry skin.
Bark: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which work together to calm inflammation and reduce dryness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried mallow flowers malva sylvestris for a quantity of one ounce.
Combine one teaspoon of dried mallow flowers with one cup of boiling water and steep for five minutes.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh strainer or cheesecloth into a large mug or teapot.
Add honey to taste and stir well to combine with the herbal infusion liquid.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before serving and enjoying warm or at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow teas helps with dry skin because it is rich in mucilage, a soothing and protective substance that forms a barrier on the skin's surface. This natural barrier traps moisture, reducing water loss and leaving skin feeling soft, supple, and hydrated. As the tea is absorbed into the skin, its anti-inflammatory properties also calm irritated and inflamed areas, providing long-lasting relief from dryness and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Mucilage: Marshmallow tea contains a high amount of mucilage, a thick, protective substance that soothes and moisturizes the skin, helping to lock in moisture and reduce dryness.
Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in marshmallow tea help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to dryness, fine lines, and wrinkles.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Marshmallow tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce redness, itching, and inflammation associated with dry skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Marshmallow roots contain mucilages, which help to soothe and calm irritated skin, making it an effective ingredient in teas for treating dry skin.
Bark: The bark of the marshmallow plant contains saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce redness and irritation associated with dry skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality althaea officinalis roots in spring or early summer for optimal flavor.
Rinse the harvested roots with cold water to remove any dirt or debris then chop them coarsely.
Combine one tablespoon of chopped althaea officinalis roots with one cup of boiling water in a saucepan.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes then strain it into a teapot or thermos using a fine-mesh sieve.
Add honey to taste and serve the tea hot or cold after preparing.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Great mullein teas helps with dry skin because of its impressive emollient and anti-inflammatory properties. The tea's active compounds, including flavonoids and saponins, work to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and flakiness. Additionally, mullein's natural moisturizing abilities help to lock in hydration, leaving skin feeling soft, supple, and comforted. This makes it an excellent addition to a skincare routine for those struggling with dry, itchy skin.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Mullein tea has anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce inflammation and redness associated with dry, itchy skin conditions like eczema and dermatitis.

Emollient and moisturizing properties: The mucilage found in mullein leaves acts as a natural emollient, providing hydration and soothing relief to dry, cracked skin by locking in moisture and reducing water loss.

Antioxidant and antimicrobial properties: Mullein tea's antioxidant and antimicrobial compounds help protect the skin from environmental stressors and infections that can exacerbate dry skin conditions, promoting overall skin health and resilience.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used in tea form to provide relief from dry, itchy skin due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
Bark: The bark is used to create a soothing and calming tea that helps to reduce inflammation and irritation associated with dry skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried mullein leaves and flowers from midsummer to fall when plant is flowering abundantly.
Chop 2-3 tablespoons of dried mullein into smaller pieces or crush 4-6 cups of fresh leaves in mortar pestle.
Combine chopped or crushed mullein with boiling water in a saucepan bringing mixture to a boil then reducing heat simmering for 10-15 minutes.
Strain tea through cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into cup or teapot discarding solids and serving immediately hot or chilled sweetened or unsweetened.
Store cooled tea in refrigerator for up to 3 days or freeze individual servings in ice cube trays for later use as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain teas helps with dry skin because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated skin. The tea's antioxidants also work to repair and regenerate damaged skin cells, promoting healthy moisture retention. Additionally, plantain teas have natural emollient properties that help to lock in moisture, leaving skin feeling soft, supple, and hydrated. Regular consumption can lead to a significant improvement in dry skin symptoms, such as itchiness, flakiness, and tightness.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Plantain tea contains compounds that reduce inflammation, which can alleviate redness, itching, and discomfort associated with dry skin.
Antioxidant properties: Plantain tea's antioxidants help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to dryness and rough texture.
Emollient properties: Plantain tea's mucilages (thick, gel-like substances) soothe and moisturize the skin, providing a protective barrier that helps lock in moisture and calm dry, itchy skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: Plantain roots have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing and calming dry skin.
Bark: The bark of the plantain plant contains compounds that help to lock in moisture and reduce inflammation, making it a popular choice for dry skin tea.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried leaves of plantago major and clean them thoroughly.
Measure out one teaspoon of dried leaves or two teaspoons of fresh leaves for every eight ounces of water.
Heat filtered water in a saucepan to a simmer over medium-low heat for 5 minutes.
Add the plantain leaves to the hot water and steep for 10-15 minutes or longer for stronger tea.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup and discard solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold teas helps with dry skin because it contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm irritated skin. The tea's natural emollients moisturize and hydrate the skin, reducing flakiness and itchiness. Additionally, its antiseptic properties help to prevent infection and promote wound healing, making it a valuable remedy for dry, cracked skin on hands, feet, and other areas of the body.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Pot marigold tea's antioxidants help to neutralize free radicals, which can damage skin cells and contribute to dryness and fine lines, promoting healthier, more hydrated skin.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in pot marigold tea help to reduce redness and irritation associated with dry skin, while also soothing and calming irritated skin.
Moisturizing properties: Pot marigold tea's flavonoids and mucilages provide a protective barrier on the skin's surface, locking in moisture and helping to soften and hydrate dry skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, the roots are effective in soothing and moisturizing dry skin.
Leaves: Containing flavonoids and phenolic acids, the leaves help to improve skin elasticity and reduce inflammation associated with dryness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried calendula flowers in amounts of one to two tablespoons per cup of boiling water.
Measure one teaspoon of dried calendula flowers or three teaspoons of fresh petals for every eight ounces of boiling water.
Combine the measured ingredients with boiling water in a heat-resistant teapot and allow it to steep for five to ten minutes.
Strain the tea into cups using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard the solids.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before serving, garnishing with lemon slices if desired.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey teas helps with dry skin because its high concentration of allantoin, a natural moisturizer, gently soothes and hydrates the skin. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties also reduce redness and irritation, while its antioxidant compounds help to protect the skin from environmental stressors that can cause dryness. Regular consumption of comfrey teas may leave skin feeling soft, supple, and revitalized, making it an excellent natural remedy for addressing dry skin concerns.
remedy_properties: Allantoin: This compound has been shown to stimulate collagen synthesis, promote wound healing, and improve skin elasticity, helping to soften and soothe dry, rough skin.

Inulin: As a prebiotic, Inulin helps maintain the health of the gut microbiome, which is linked to overall skin health; a healthy gut-skin axis can help reduce inflammation and irritation that can contribute to dry skin.

Saponins: Saponins have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help calm and soothe dry, itchy skin by reducing redness and swelling, while also promoting the growth of new skin cells to replace damaged ones.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, comfrey roots help soothe and hydrate dry skin by creating a protective barrier.
Rhyzomes: The thick, underground stems of comfrey rhyzomes contain anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce redness and itching associated with dry skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh comfrey leaves and flowers or use dried herbs in equal parts of 20-30 grams.
Rinse the comfrey mixture with cold running water to remove any debris or impurities gently.
Combine the comfrey mixture with 250-500 milliliters of boiling water in a large glass jar quietly.
Allow the tea to steep for 10-15 minutes, then strain and discard solids completely.
Store the resulting liquid tea in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to three days quickly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Large-leaved lime teas helps with dry skin because of its high concentration of antioxidants, flavonoids, and limonoids. These compounds work to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing inflammation and redness associated with dryness. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties also help to lock in moisture, leaving the skin feeling soft, supple, and hydrated. Additionally, the lime's natural astringent properties help to gently tighten pores and balance the skin's pH, further reducing the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles.
remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Large-leaved lime tea contains high amounts of antioxidants, which help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress that can exacerbate dry skin.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: The tea's anti-inflammatory properties, particularly those containing flavonoids and phenolic acids, help to reduce redness and itching associated with dry skin, while also soothing and calming irritated skin.

Moisturizing saponins: Large-leaved lime tea contains saponins, which are natural emollients that help to lock in moisture, soften and soothe dry skin, and improve its overall hydration and elasticity.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used in medicinal teas for dry skin due to their high content of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help soothe and calm irritated skin.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the large-leaved lime plant are used in teas as they contain mucilages that help lock in moisture and provide a protective barrier for dry skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried large-leaved lime flowers in the morning when their fragrance is most potent.
Combine one teaspoon of dried flowers or two teaspoons of fresh flowers with one cup of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for five to seven minutes allowing the flavors and aromas to meld together harmoniously.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a teapot or cup discarding the solids carefully.
Serve the tea hot or chilled as desired sweetening with honey if preferred and enjoying within six hours of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Littleleaf linden teas helps with dry skin because it is rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which work to soothe and moisturize the skin. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties also help to reduce redness and irritation, while its emollient properties soften and calm dry, tight skin. Additionally, the tea's humectant qualities attract and retain moisture, leaving skin feeling supple and hydrated. Overall, littleleaf linden teas provides a natural and gentle approach to alleviating dry, itchy skin.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Littleleaf linden tea has anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids, which help to reduce redness and inflammation associated with dry skin.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in littleleaf linden tea, such as phenolic acids, help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy skin cell turnover and reducing the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles.
Soothing and moisturizing properties: The saponins and mucilages in littleleaf linden tea create a soothing and protective barrier on the skin's surface, helping to lock in moisture and calm dryness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and flavonoids, which help to moisturize and soothe dry skin.
Bark: Contains tannins that can help to reduce inflammation and irritation associated with dry skin.
remedy_recipe: Gather one teaspoon of dried littleleaf linden flowers for every eight ounces of boiling water.
Steep the dried flowers in boiling water for five to seven minutes or until desired strength is reached.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a cup or teapot.
Add honey or lemon to taste if desired and allow the tea to cool slightly.
Serve immediately and enjoy the soothing and calming effects of the herbal littleleaf linden tea.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: German chamomile teas helps with dry skin because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated skin. The apigenin content in chamomile tea has been shown to reduce inflammation and promote hydration, leaving skin feeling soft and supple. Additionally, the antioxidants present in chamomile tea help to protect the skin from environmental stressors and damage caused by free radicals, leading to a more radiant and healthy-looking complexion.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: German Chamomile tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like apigenin and luteolin, which can reduce inflammation in the skin, helping to soothe and calm dry, itchy, or irritated skin.

Antioxidant properties: Chamomile tea's antioxidant properties help protect the skin from free radical damage, which can contribute to dryness and premature aging, promoting healthier, more resilient skin.

Soothing flavonoids: German Chamomile contains soothing flavonoids like quercetin, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and improve skin hydration, helping to alleviate dryness and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and soothing compounds, chamomile flowers calm and moisturize dry skin, reducing inflammation and itchiness.
Roots: Containing apigenin and other flavonoids, chamomile roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and protect dry, irritated skin.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dried chamomile flowers measuring one to two teaspoons per cup of boiling water.
Boil fresh water in a tea kettle or pot and pour it over the dried chamomile flowers.
Steep the mixture for five to seven minutes allowing the herbs to release their natural oils and flavors.
Strain the tea into a cup using a tea infuser or fine-mesh sieve to remove the solids.
Serve the warm or cooled tea immediately or store it in an airtight container for up to two days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow teas helps with dry skin because of its unique ability to nourish and soothe the skin. The antioxidants and flavonoids present in yarrow tea work to lock in moisture, reducing the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles while hydrating the skin. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of yarrow tea help to calm irritated skin, reducing redness and itchiness.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and Anti-inflammatory compounds: Yarrow tea's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to dry skin, by neutralizing free radicals and reducing redness and irritation.
Saponins: Saponins in yarrow tea have emollient and moisturizing effects, helping to soothe and calm dry, irritated skin by creating a protective barrier and locking in moisture.

Tannins: Tannins in yarrow tea have astringent properties that help reduce skin inflammation and improve skin tone by constricting blood vessels and reducing sebum production, which can contribute to dryness and flakiness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the yarrow plant contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm irritated dry skin, making them a great addition to medicinal teas.

Flowers: The flowers of the yarrow plant are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids that help protect the skin from environmental stressors and promote healthy skin cell growth, making them ideal for soothing dry skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried yarrow flowers achillea millefolium leaves and stems.
Combine 1 teaspoon of dried yarrow herbs with boiling water to steep for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup or teapot.
Add honey or lemon to taste if desired and enjoy the warm comforting tea.
Store any leftover tea in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat dry skin with herbal teas is by using them as a compress or in the bath. Steep a strong tea from herbs known to soothe and moisturize, such as chamomile, calendula, or lavender, and let it cool. For a compress, soak a clean cloth in the tea and apply to the affected areas. For a bath, add several tea bags or loose tea to the running water. These herbal teas help restore the skin's natural barrier, reducing dryness and leaving skin soft and supple.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to dry skin that are treated with herbal teas include eczema and dandruff. Chamomile tea is often used as a compress to soothe eczema symptoms, while nettle tea can be consumed to alleviate internal inflammation that may contribute to skin conditions. For dandruff, sage tea can be used as a rinse to soothe an itchy scalp and combat flaking. Additionally, burdock root tea can help improve liver function, which can in turn promote healthy skin. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare professional before starting any new treatment regimen.
